question: Does sentence-level fact-bearing retrieval let entailment beat the incumbent lexical screen through the real gate? (the pre-registered test of 'grounding is retrieval-bound')

c1_wikipedia_arm.note: faithful to the frozen protocol (C1 groundable subset + keyless Wikipedia sentence retrieval)
c1_wikipedia_arm.interpretation: directionally strong (NLI -0.09 on metadata -> +0.17 on sentences; over-abstention 0.05->0.0) but UNDERPOWERED (n=18, CI includes 0). Not self-authored-extended (per protocol).

fever_gate_arm.note: power-adequate resolution: FEVER gold sentence evidence through the SAME gate (public benchmark, not self-authored)
fever_gate_arm.caveat: the lexical incumbent is very brittle on FEVER's paraphrastic evidence (coverage 0.003) — part of NLI's win is token-overlap failing on paraphrase; the metadata<->sentence contrast is the robust signal.

verdict: GO — RETRIEVAL-BOUND DIAGNOSIS CONFIRMED. Entailment beats the incumbent lexical screen through the real gate IFF evidence is fact-bearing sentences: metadata evidence (rung 5) NLI -0.09 (loses, over-abstains); sentence evidence (FEVER n=400) NLI +0.142, CI [0.08,0.19] excludes 0 (wins, no over-abstention). The mechanism works given good evidence; the production blocker is retrieval/evidence quality, not the verifier head.
preDeclaredNext: per the maintainer-AI's outcome table: GO -> fund D (retrieval as the product) with NLI as the escalation-tier verifier. D is a roadmap redirection -> maintainer decision.
